channel.title: NPR Topics: National
channel.description: NPR coverage of national news, U.S. politics, elections, business, arts, culture, health and science, and technology. Subscribe to the NPR Nation RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Wyoming lawmakers use pro-natalist arguments to justify proposed new partial abortion ban
item.description: Wyoming lawmakers say tight new restrictions on abortion are needed to grow the state's population. Demographers and others say economic conditions are more to blame for an exodus of young people.
item.content:encoded:
Wyoming lawmakers say tight new restrictions on abortion are needed to grow the state's population. Demographers and others say economic conditions are more to blame for an exodus of young people.

(Image credit: Jamie Schwaberow)
item.dc:creator: Hanna Merzbach

item.title: Hotels have a big World Cup problem: Bookings are running far below projections
item.description: Nearly 80% of hotels surveyed by a body representing the industry reported bookings were running below projections — a worrisome sign with only weeks to go before the World Cup.
item.content:encoded:
Nearly 80% of hotels surveyed by a body representing the industry reported bookings were running below projections — a worrisome sign with only weeks to go before the World Cup.

(Image credit: Jamie Squire)
item.dc:creator: Rafael Nam

item.title: More college applicants are opting to include SAT or ACT scores in their submissions
item.description: This application cycle, way more students reported test scores, up 10% compared with last year. That's despite many colleges having "test-optional" admissions policies.
item.content:encoded: This application cycle, way more students reported test scores, up 10% compared with last year. That's despite many colleges having "test-optional" admissions policies.
item.dc:creator: JD Allen

item.title: A United jet struck a light pole and a truck near Newark airport, police say
item.description: New Jersey State Police say a United Airlines passenger jet struck a light pole and damaged a truck as it was coming in for a landing at Newark Liberty International Airport on Sunday.
item.content:encoded:
New Jersey State Police say a United Airlines passenger jet struck a light pole and damaged a truck as it was coming in for a landing at Newark Liberty International Airport on Sunday.

(Image credit: Kena Betancur)
item.dc:creator: Joel Rose

item.title: The U.S. fights to reopen the Strait of Hormuz as UAE says it's attacked by Iran
item.description: The U.S. military said it battled Iranian forces and sank six small boats as it moved to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, while the United Arab Emirates said Iran fired missiles and drones its way.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. military said it battled Iranian forces and sank six small boats as it moved to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, while the United Arab Emirates said Iran fired missiles and drones its way.

(Image credit: Amirhosein Khorgooi)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: The many ways Trump wants to change D.C., from buildings to statues to parks
item.description: Many of the changes Trump wants to make to the White House and the city are facing legal challenges. Some are reversible, while others could reshape the nation's capital for generations to come.
item.content:encoded:
Many of the changes Trump wants to make to the White House and the city are facing legal challenges. Some are reversible, while others could reshape the nation's capital for generations to come.

(Image credit: Tom Brenner, Rahmat Gul, Mark Schiefelbein, Jon Elswick)
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: Louisiana lawmakers working to pass map that could eliminate majority Black districts
item.description: Primary elections have started in Louisiana, but votes cast in congressional races may not count as the Republican legislature is redrawing district lines to get one more Republican seat.
item.content:encoded: Primary elections have started in Louisiana, but votes cast in congressional races may not count as the Republican legislature is redrawing district lines to get one more Republican seat.
item.dc:creator: Leila Fadel

item.title: How the SCOTUS voting rights ruling could affect Congress, according to Eric Holder
item.description: Eric Holder, chairman of the National Democratic Redistricting Committee, talks with NPR's Michel Martin about a Supreme Court ruling that could pave the way for a drop in Black representation in Congress.
item.content:encoded:
Eric Holder, chairman of the National Democratic Redistricting Committee, talks with NPR's Michel Martin about a Supreme Court ruling that could pave the way for a drop in Black representation in Congress.

(Image credit: Toya Sarno Jordan/Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: Bill aims to stop 'claim sharks' from targeting disabled vets after NPR investigation
item.description: The bipartisan bill would block companies from using auto-dialers to harvest private information about disabled veterans. The firms charge veterans for a service lawmakers say is supposed to be free.
item.content:encoded: The bipartisan bill would block companies from using auto-dialers to harvest private information about disabled veterans. The firms charge veterans for a service lawmakers say is supposed to be free.
item.dc:creator: Caley Fox Shannon

item.title: Trump's power of political retribution will be tested this week in Indiana primary
item.description: President Trump has thrown his support behind challengers to Republican state senators who opposed his redistricting push.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump has thrown his support behind challengers to Republican state senators who opposed his redistricting push.

(Image credit: Tamara Keith)
item.dc:creator: Tamara Keith
